etymology.description: semantic 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") + phonetic 久(久) (jiǔ) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The character 灸 (jiǔ, "moxibustion") combines the semantic 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") with the phonetic 久(久) (jiǔ) to signify the practice of applying fire or heat, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The 火(火) component directly refers to the application of heat, while 久(久) helps to provide the pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 她最近在学习灸法(她最近在學習灸法)。
examples.english: She has recently been studying the art of moxibustion.

examples.chinese: 医生建议我试试灸疗(醫生建議我試試灸療)，来缓解腰痛(來緩解腰痛)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiànyì wǒ shìshì jiǔliáo, lái huǎnjiě yāotòng.
examples.english: The doctor suggested I try moxibustion to alleviate back pain.

examples.chinese: 在中医中(在中醫中)，灸是一种常用的疗法(灸是一種常用的療法)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhōngyī zhōng, jiǔ shì yī zhǒng chángyòng de liáofǎ.
examples.english: In traditional Chinese medicine, moxibustion is a common treatment method.
